resources.string:
"
 
V5.0.0 - Support downloadable machine learning models. 
 First-Use setup wizard. 
 UI Improvements. 
 Significant modernisation of underlying code. 
V4.3.1 - Fixed user interface issues on Android-15 and Android-16 
V4.3.0 - Added support for Android 15 (API 35) and above. 
 - Simplified data sharing editor by grouping events for editing. "

Please enable the new Data Sharing feature to help improve OpenSeizureDetector!
(see below for details)

Please subscribe to updates at www.openseizuredetector.org.uk so we can inform you of any issues.

Changes in this version:

For details of how OpenSeizure Detector collects and uses your personal data, please refer to the Privacy Policy below.

Please enable Data Sharing to help improve OpenSeizureDetector!
(see below for details)

Please subscribe to updates at www.openseizuredetector.org.uk so we can inform you of any issues.
Sorry, OpenSeizureDetector Has Crashed. Please Email this log file to us so we can work out what happened and fix it.\nThanks, Graham.

Dear OpenSeizureDetector,

Application is just crashed, please check following error log for more details.
OpenSeizureDetector (Using UCE Handler\nCopyright © 2018 Rohit Sahebrao Surwase.)
Problem with SMS Permissions\n(please check phone settings -> Apps -> OpenSeizureDetector -> Permissions
Enable Advanced Settings (exposes a lot more things you can change....)
Auto start OpenSeizureDetector when phone is started
Select whether to use a Garmin Watch, Pebble Watch or network connection as the seizure detector data source.
Log Data to Central OpenSeizureDetector Database
Use mobile internet to log remote data
Alarm Ratio Threshold (Default = 57). Increase to reduce sensitivity.
Treat a Null heart rate as an alarm condition

OpenSeizureDetector epileptic
 (tonic-clonic) seizure detector and alarm system. Uses a Smart watch to detect the shaking associated with a seizure, then raises audible and text message (SMS) location alerts for carers.

 The system is free and open source - see openseizuredetector.org.uk for details.

 Please report any issues by raising an issue on the source code repository - https://github.com/OpenSeizureDetector/Android_Pebble_SD/issues or email graham@openseizuredetector.org.uk

 Android App and Garmin Watch App, copyright Graham Jones, 2015-2022.
Cancel Audible Active - not sending SMS
Network State Changed - no Wifi Connection
Network State Changed - No Active Network
Problem Parsing Preferences - Something won\'t work - Please go back to Settings and correct it!
ERROR: FAILED TO SEND SMS MESSAGE
SMS Alarms Disabled - not doing anything!
OpenSeizureDetector Dialer App Not installed - Required for Phone Call Alerts.
Select Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Device to provide seizure (acceleration and heart rate) data) when using the Bluetooth Device data source.
Select the seizure detector data source to use.
Log Alarm events to Phone Storage
Use mobile internet to share data
Wearer ID of the person wearing the watch (from OSD Web API)
Prevent the screen from blanking while the application is running.
App Version Number - used to decide whether to display the welcome message or not.
Require manual reset of alarms to reset them to silence them.
Duration in seconds that alarms will initiate for before being silenced automatically.
Issue an audible alarm if the seizure detector enters an alarm condition.
Issue an audible alarm if the seizure detector enters a warning (pre-alarm) condition.
Issue an audible alarm if the system detects a fault (e.g. can not talk to watch).
Duration that fault alarms are muted before initiating.
Check that data arrives at the expected frequency (5±1 seconds) and raise a fault if not.
Play an MP3 file to create the alarm beeps rather than using the default tone generator.
Issue a SMS (Text Message) alarm if the seizure detector enters an alarm condition. NOTE: THE MESSAGE WILL INCLUDE THE USER\'s LOCATION. Disable this option if sharing your location is a concern.
Telephone number(s) to notify by SMS of Alarm (comma separated).
Message to be Sent by SMS when a Seizure is Detected.
Period between server data requests in miliseconds.
MAC Address of BLE Data Source Device
Name of BLE Data Source Device
Time period at which data is sent to the phone (does not affect analysis frequency - see Sample Period setting for that)
Time that alarms are muted following a long press of the UP button
Time that manual alarms sound following a long press of the DOWN button
Time to wait before initiating warning (Default = 5 sec)
Time to wait before initiating alarm (Default = 10 sec)
Alarm Ratio Threshold (Default = 50). Increase this value to reduce sensitivity if false alarms are a problem.
Select one of the three available modes of operation.
Higher Frequency is more Accurate, but uses more battery power.
Set Debug mode on or off.
Period (seconds) that we wait for data from the watch before assuming the watch app is not running and re-starting it.
Period (sec) we wait for data before restarting watch app.
Set Display Spectrum mode on or off.
Problem Parsing Preferences - Something won\'t work - Please go back to Settings and correct it!
SMS Alarm already sent - not re-sending
SMS Alarms Disabled - not sending SMS Alert
ERROR - OpenSizureDetector did not restart properly - please Force Stop OpenSeizureDetector or re-boot phone.
ERROR - Permission for SMS or Location Denied - Not Sending SMS
Time to wait before initiating warning (Default = 5 sec)
Time to wait before initiating alarm (Default = 10 sec)
Generate alarm if low Blood Oxygen Saturation is detected
Treat an error condition (null value of oxygen saturation reading) as an alarm condition
Log in to OpenSeizureDetector Data Sharing
Number of Events Stored on Phone:
Datapoint not found - not doing anything
Settings that control how data is recorded on the phone and uploaded to the Open Seizure Database
The time (in seconds) before and after a seizure event that we record data.
The period (in days) that data will be retained and is protected from deletion by the \'Prune Database\' Option.
Automatically Prune (Trim) the Database periodically to prevent excessive storage capacity (memory) usage.
The period (in seconds) between attempts to upload data to the remote server. Each attempt only uploads a single event, not all the available data.
Please Check your Data Sharing Events

WARNING: The Android System Battery Optimisation Settings are configured to 'Optimise' OpenSeizureDetector.

 This means it is likely to be shutdown when operating on battery power, so will not work correctly

 Please go into the Phone Settings and select Apps->OpenSeizureDetector->Battery->Optimise battery usage and set it so that OpenSeizureDetector is NOT optimised (on Older versions of Andoroid this option is in
Please select the events highlighted in pink to say if they are real seizures or false alarms
ERROR: OpenSeizureDetector Server is not running - please re-start it

Please register for Data Sharing and Log in using the App menu or button below

 This will help with developing OpenSeizureDetector to increase the detection reliability and reduce the false alarm rate

 If you would like more information about the data sharing system and privacy policy, please see the Data Sharing page (https://www.openseizuredetector.org.uk/?page_id=1818) 
 on the https://openseizuredetector.org.uk web site.

OpenSeizureDetector needs permission to read your phone state and send SMS messages to enable it to send SMS alerts when it detects a seizure.

 With this permission enabled the App will send an SMS message to the phone numbers you enter on th Alarm settings page of the app when it detects a seizure.

 Please grant the permissions in the following screens after pressing 'OK' below....

 You can change this later by going into the phone settings and selecting Apps->OpenSeizureDetector->Permissions

OpenSeizureDetector collects location data to enable the SMS Location Alarms feature to include your location in the alarm message when the app is closed or not in use.

 This means that it will be able to send SMS text messages containing your location to the phone numbers you enter on the Alarm settings page of the app when it detects a seizure, by running in the background, even when the app is closed.
 It does not store your location or share it with anyone other than the phone numbers you specify


Please grant the permissions in the following screen by selecting While using the app if you wish to use SMS Location Alarms

 You can change this later by going into the phone settings and selecting Apps->OpenSeizureDetector->Permissions.

OpenSeizureDetector collects location data to enable the SMS Location Alarms feature to include your location in the alarm message when the app is closed or not in use.

 This means that it will be able to send SMS text messages containing your location to the phone numbers you enter on the Alarm settings page of the app when it detects a seizure, by running in the background, even when the app is closed.
 It does not store your location or share it with anyone other than the phone numbers you specify.

 Please grant the required permission by selecting Allow all the time on the next screen, if you wish to use SMS Location Alarms

 You can change this later by going into the phone settings and selecting Apps->OpenSeizureDetector->Permissions.
Mark All Unverified Events as Unknown?
Please confirm that all genuine seizure events have been verified before marking all unverified events as type \'unknown\'. \n\nContinue to mark unverified events as Unknown?
Not Logged in to Data Sharing
You must be logged in to the Data Sharing system to be able to report seizures.
Original deterministic OpenSeizureDetector Algorithm to generate alarms. ***Disabling this algorithm is NOT recommended except for testing purposes***
Machine Learning / Artificial Intelligence / Neural Network algorithm to generate alarms.
Mark all Unverified Events as False Alarm
Please confirm that all genuine seizure events have been verified before marking all unverified events as type \'False Alarm\'. \n\nContinue to mark unverified events as False Alarm?
Mark all Unverified Events as False Alarm
Continuously log data to the data sharing system to provide background \'normal daily activity\' data to help reduce false alarms.
Start Normal Daily Activity (NDA) Logging (will stop automatically after 24 hours)?
Do not display the battery optimisation dialog on start-up (some phones report that OpenSeizureDetector is optimised, even if it is not, so the dialog is a nuissance)
SMS Alarm already sent - not re-sending
SmsTimer.onFinish - mLocationFinder is null - this should not happen! - Please report this issue!
Error uploading event - date is null
This will remove all data from the database that is more than %d days old.\nThis can NOT be undone.\nAre you sure?
Enable Adaptive Threshold Heart Rate Alarm
Window Size for Adaptive Threshold Calculation
Moving Average Time Window (seconds) must be a multiple of 5 seconds
The alarm threshold offset from the average measured Heart Rate (bpm)
Enable Rolling Average Heart Rate Alarm
Lower Heart Rate Threshold for Average Heart Rate Alarm
Lower Threshold for Average Heart Rate Alarm
Upper Heart Rate Threshold for Average Heart Rate Alarm
Upper Threshold for Average Heart Rate Alarm
Window size for Average Heart Rate Calculation
Window size (in seconds) for Average Heart Rate Calculation - must be a factor of 5 seconds.
Produce a fault warning if the heart rate measurement freezes and does not change for more than 1 minute.
Movement Threshold (% standard deviation) to activate the Machine Learning Algorithm
Minimum seizure probability (%) required for the ML algorithm to trigger an alarm
ID number of machine learning (AI) model to be used - users should not edit this, but use the model Manager page instead.
Download and select a machine learning model for seizure detection
Generates a fault if no movement has been detected for a specified period (signifying the watch has been removed)
The threshold (as % standard deviation) applied to each set of accelerometer data to determine if a \'Fidget\' has occurred.
A fault is generated if no movement (fidgets) are detected for more than the Fidget Detector Period.
Bring the Main Activity to the foreground when an alarm occurs (if enabled).

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nam in scelerisque sem. Mauris volutpat, dolor id interdum ullamcorper, risus dolor egestas lectus, sit amet mattis purus dui nec risus. Maecenas non sodales nisi, vel dictum dolor. Class aptent taciti sociosqu ad litora torquent per conubia nostra, per inceptos himenaeos. Suspendisse blandit eleifend diam, vel rutrum tellus vulputate quis. Aliquam eget libero aliquet, imperdiet nisl a, ornare ex. Sed rhoncus est ut libero porta lobortis. Fusce in dictum tellus.

 Suspendisse interdum ornare ante. Aliquam nec cursus lorem. Morbi id magna felis. Vivamus egestas, est a condimentum egestas, turpis nisl iaculis ipsum, in dictum tellus dolor sed neque. Morbi tellus erat, dapibus ut sem a, iaculis tincidunt dui. Interdum et malesuada fames ac ante ipsum primis in faucibus. Curabitur et eros porttitor, ultricies urna vitae, molestie nibh. Phasellus at commodo eros, non aliquet metus. Sed maximus nisl nec dolor bibendum, vel congue leo egestas.

 Sed interdum tortor nibh, in sagittis risus mollis quis. Curabitur mi odio, condimentum sit amet auctor at, mollis non turpis. Nullam pretium libero vestibulum, finibus orci vel, molestie quam. Fusce blandit tincidunt nulla, quis sollicitudin libero facilisis et. Integer interdum nunc ligula, et fermentum metus hendrerit id. Vestibulum lectus felis, dictum at lacinia sit amet, tristique id quam. Cras eu consequat dui. Suspendisse sodales nunc ligula, in lobortis sem porta sed. Integer id ultrices magna, in luctus elit. Sed a pellentesque est.

 Aenean nunc velit, lacinia sed dolor sed, ultrices viverra nulla. Etiam a venenatis nibh. Morbi laoreet, tortor sed facilisis varius, nibh orci rhoncus nulla, id elementum leo dui non lorem. Nam mollis ipsum quis auctor varius. Quisque elementum eu libero sed commodo. In eros nisl, imperdiet vel imperdiet et, scelerisque a mauris. Pellentesque varius ex nunc, quis imperdiet eros placerat ac. Duis finibus orci et est auctor tincidunt. Sed non viverra ipsum. Nunc quis augue egestas, cursus lorem at, molestie sem. Morbi a consectetur ipsum, a placerat diam. Etiam vulputate dignissim convallis. Integer faucibus mauris sit amet finibus convallis.

 Phasellus in aliquet mi. Pellentesque habitant morbi tristique senectus et netus et malesuada fames ac turpis egestas. In volutpat arcu ut felis sagittis, in finibus massa gravida. Pellentesque id tellus orci. Integer dictum, lorem sed efficitur ullamcorper, libero justo consectetur ipsum, in mollis nisl ex sed nisl. Donec maximus ullamcorper sodales. Praesent bibendum rhoncus tellus nec feugiat. In a ornare nulla. Donec rhoncus libero vel nunc consequat, quis tincidunt nisl eleifend. Cras bibendum enim a justo luctus vestibulum. Fusce dictum libero quis erat maximus, vitae volutpat diam dignissim.

NOTEs:\n 1: Devices will not appear on this list if they are already connected - disconnect device from GadgetBridge etc. to allow it to be selected here. \n 2: The PineTime bluetooth radio might go to sleep when not connected - press the button on the PineTime watch to wake it up to make sure it is detected.
Bluetooth permissions are required to communicate with the bluetooth (BLE) data source
ERROR: failed to create local event
ERROR: Failed to start Log Manager
Permission required to analyse your movements to detect seizure activity.
The number of seconds to delay between an alarm initiating and an SMS message being sent (default=10)
How often to check for new recommended ML models

A new recommended machine learning model is available:

%1$s

Would you like to download it now?
Enable running multiple ML models simultaneously for voting
How many ML models to use (1-5)
How to combine results from multiple algorithms
Log file exported to Downloads folder
Choose the minimum logcat level mirrored to persistent system log files.
This wizard will help you configure OpenSeizureDetector for your needs.

You will be asked to:

• Choose your data source
• Configure the data source
• Select seizure detection algorithms

You can change these settings later in the Settings menu.
Select where seizure detection data will come from:
Uses phone\'s accelerometer for testing. Not suitable for real seizure detection.
Uses a low-cost PineTime watch for reliable seizure detection.
Uses a Garmin smart watch for reliable seizure detection and heart rate monitoring.
Connect to another device running OpenSeizureDetector for remote alarm annunciation.
Choose which seizure detection algorithms to use. You can enable multiple algorithms.

Machine Learning (\'AI\') detection.\nGives good seizure detection reliability with fewer false alarms than the original OSD Algorithm.\nFalse alarm performance should improve over time as users report false alarms and real seizures using our \'Data Sharing\' facility.
Detects abnormal heart rate patterns. Currently requires Garmin watch for reliable heart rate measurement.

Original OpenSeizureDetector Deterministic Algorithm (OSD)\nProven tonic-clonic seizure detection - good for night time use, but gives false alarms for some normal activities that involve repetitive movements (brushing teeth, washing dishes etc.).
Enhanced OSD algorithm that detects arm flapping movements for maximum night time tonic-clonic seizure detection performance.
Note: At least one algorithm should be enabled for seizure detection to work.
You can change these settings at any time from the Settings menu.\n\nPress \'Get Started\' to begin using OpenSeizureDetector.
Checking if PineTime Updater app is installed...
✗ PineTime Updater app is not installed. Please install it from the Google Play Store using the button above, then press the back button to return to this screen.
2. Install OpenSeizureDetector Firmware onto PineTime Watch

Install the custom OpenSeizureDetector firmware onto the PineTime watch, so that it will collect and send the data needed for seizure detection.\nNote: You will be asked to grant the permissions needed to access the watch over bluetooth and display a notification to show progress during the update.
3. Connect your PineTime watch to OpenSeizureDetector via Bluetooth
Next, we\'ll configure the seizure detection algorithm.

Demo mode uses your phone\'s built-in accelerometer to detect movement to demonstrate OpenSeizureDetector. This is useful for testing the app\'s functionality, but it MUST NOT be used as a seizure detector in this mode.\n\nWhen the main app starts, you will be asked for permissions about monitoring activity in order to enable access to the accelerometer.

⚠ Demo mode is NOT suitable for real seizure detection. The phone\'s accelerometer cannot reliably detect seizures when the phone is not attached to the wrist.\n\nFor real seizure detection, please use a wrist watch (PineTime or Garmin).
To switch to a different data source later, go to Settings menu in the main app screen after this wizard completes.
Next, we\'ll configure the seizure detection algorithm.

• Connects to another device running OpenSeizureDetector to generate Alarm and Warning Notifications

• This device will receive alarm notifications from the remote device

• Both devices must be on the same WiFi network

• Useful if you can not hear the main OpenSeizureDevice alarms from another part of the house

To find the remote device\'s IP address, look on the \'System\' tab of the main OpenSeizureDetector screen for\n\'Access Server at: http://xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:8080\',\nor check your router\'s client list.
Enter the IP address of the OpenSeizureDetector device above
Your selected data source may require additional configuration that cannot be completed in this wizard.

1. Finish this wizard by tapping 'Get Started'
2. Go to Settings in the main application
3. Look for 'Data Source' or 'Bluetooth' settings
4. Complete any additional setup steps there

For detailed instructions on configuring your specific device, please visit:

https://github.com/OpenSeizureDetector/OpenSeizureDetector

You can also contact our support team through the app's settings.
Next, we\'ll configure the seizure detection algorithm.
Set up your Garmin watch for seizure detection - refer to the \'Garmin Seizure Detector\' page on the OpenSeizureDetector.org.uk web site for detailed instructions
1. Pair the watch with your phone using the Garmin Connect app as per the Garmin instructions.
2. Download the GarminSD watch app file from the OpenSeizureDetector source code repository onto a computer (the installation can not be carried out on a phone)
3. Connect your watch to the computer via USB.
4. Copy the watch app file from the computer onto the watch.
5. Launch the OpenSeizureDetector app on your watch
• Make sure the Garmin watch app is running before continuing
Next, we\'ll configure the seizure detection algorithm.
Enter the IP address of the OpenSeizureDetector device
Please enter a valid IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.100)
PineTime Updater not installed. Install from Play Store.

✗ Cannot reach server. Please check:
• IP address is correct
• Server is running
• Device is on the same WiFi network
OpenSeizureDetector could not start monitoring — please restart the app or reboot your phone.

OpenSeizureDetector could not start its background monitoring service. This can happen if Android has reached its daily limit for this type of background service. Please restart the app. If the problem persists, reboot your phone.
